content: NPI evidence assessment challenges: randomized trials often infeasible, reliance on observational studies with confounding, varying compliance levels, and simultaneous implementation of multiple NPIs making attribution difficult. Systematic reviews show: mask mandates associated with reduced transmission (adjusted OR 0.5-0.8); ventilation improvements reduce airborne transmission; school closures alone show modest effect but contribute when combined with other NPIs. Effectiveness depends on context: population density, baseline transmission, variant characteristics, and cultural acceptance. Economic and mental health impacts must be weighed against infection prevention benefits.
source: Cochrane Reviews, Nature Human Behaviour systematic analyses
